t1-wil.name: T1 - WIL Office
t1-wil.content:
Hi [Name],

I'm Harry, founder of Blues & Greens - a youth mentoring program in Melbourne that pairs teenage boys (13-17) with young male mentors in their 20s for weekly one-on-one video sessions.

I'm reaching out because I think your students would be a great fit for what we do - & we'd love to register as a placement host for your WIL program.

The mentor role is simple: one hour a week, a video call with a teenage boy. We provide full training, ongoing supervision & a structured session framework. For psych, education or youth work students, it's real hands-on experience working with adolescents - not a tick-box placement.

What makes it different: mentors are paid ($50/session), it's fully remote, & every session is real 1:1 time with a young person. It fits neatly within most placement frameworks.

Happy to send through a student-facing blurb your team can share with the cohort - or a one-pager if that's easier to circulate. Whatever works best for you.

Cheers,
Harry Quayle
Founder, Blues & Greens
harry@bluesandgreens.com.au
bluesandgreens.lovable.app/apply

subject-a.name: Subject Line A
subject-a.content: Youth mentoring opportunity for your students

subject-b.content: Paid mentoring role for [Uni] students - placements available

t6-followup.name: T6 - Follow-Up (Day 7)
t6-followup.content:
Hi [Name], just bumping this up - totally understand if you're buried in emails.

Happy to send through a student-facing blurb or a one-pager if that's easier to share with your students. Whatever works best for you.

Cheers, Harry

student-blurb.name: Student-Facing Blurb
student-blurb.content:
Blues & Greens is looking for uni students to become paid mentors for teenage boys.

The gig: one hour a week, you jump on a video call with a 13-17 year old boy. You chat, check in on his week & become a consistent positive person in his life. That's it.

You get full training before you start & ongoing support the whole way through. It's $50 per session, fully remote & fits around your schedule.

If you're studying psych, education, social work or youth work - this is real experience with young people that you genuinely can't get in a lecture theatre.

Apply here: bluesandgreens.lovable.app/apply

t2-unit-coord.name: T2 - Unit Coordinator
t2-unit-coord.content:
Hi [Name],

I'm Harry, founder of Blues & Greens - a youth mentoring program in Melbourne.

I used to get emails like this from unit coordinators when I was at uni & honestly, the opportunities that came through those emails were some of the most valuable things I did.

We're looking for young men in their 20s to become paid casual mentors for teenage boys. One hour a week, video call, full training & supervision. $50 per session.

I think this would be really relevant for your [unit name] students - it's real-world experience with adolescents that you can't get in a textbook.

Happy to send through a student-facing blurb your team can share with the cohort - or a one-pager if that's easier to circulate. Whatever works best for you.

Cheers,
Harry Quayle
Founder, Blues & Greens
harry@bluesandgreens.com.au
bluesandgreens.lovable.app/apply
